feedback: The Sprint 1 contract is testable, complete, and correct. All 23 criteria specify concrete, automatable test methods (filesystem inspection, ast static analysis, unit tests, subprocess CLI invocations, git diff). Coverage of harness/lib/utils.sh is function-by-function complete: slugify (C1-04), sprint_pad (C1-05), sprint_dir (C1-06), json_read (C1-07), file_exists (C1-08), init_harness_state (C1-09, C1-10), update_handoff (C1-11), update_regression_registry (C1-12), update_progress (C1-13), log_cost (C1-14), check_cost_cap (C1-15), and the colored log helpers (C1-16). Every numbered scenario in tests/layer1/test-utils.bats has an explicit Python equivalent listed (C1-20). Project constraints are properly encoded: C1-02 enforces 'no .sh/.ps1 modifications' via git diff, C1-03 enforces stdlib-only via ast inspection, and C1-22 explicitly addresses Windows path handling (sprint_dir returns forward slashes to match bash output, while filesystem ops go through pathlib). Test infrastructure pieces (HarnessTestCase setUp/tearDown, install_fixture, init_test_repo, run-all.py CLI) all map cleanly to F6 in the spec. Fixtures named in the criteria (config-general.json, handoff-after-sprint1.json, handoff-initial.json, registry-empty.json, contract-sprint01.json) all exist under tests/helpers/fixtures/. C1-23 (regression isolation via git status diff) is a strong guard against test pollution. No criterion contradicts the project's no-third-party-deps rule. Two minor advisory notes follow but none are blocking.
advisoryNotes: C1-20 description states '22 cases' but the breakdown listed (5+2+2+4+3+4+3+2) sums to 25, which matches the actual scenario count in test-utils.bats. The threshold '>=22' still permits 25, so this is a textual inconsistency rather than a coverage gap. Suggest the generator implement all 25 cases and note the corrected count in the implementation log.

C1-09 lists harness-state/progress.md among the four required files but does not explicitly list it in the testSteps text -- the threshold 'All four files present' implies it. Generator should ensure the unit test asserts progress.md presence as well as the other three.
C1-15 (check_cost_cap) is correctly marked non-blocking and only requires no-exception behavior, which mirrors the bash version's lightweight informational logging. Good.

C1-22 says 'grep harness/lib/utils.py for backslash literals -> none in path construction' -- the evaluator should be careful that legitimate non-path uses (e.g. ANSI '\033[' color escapes, or '\n' in format strings) are not flagged. Suggest narrowing the grep to literal path strings such as '\\harness-state' patterns rather than any backslash.
